title: Acceptance criteria for UI
use_when: Acceptance criteria for UI. Use when the user needs a product workflow for technical related to acceptance criteria for ui. Trigger terms: quality-assurance, ui, acceptance-criteria, responsive-design, technical.
do_not_use_when: Do not use Acceptance criteria for UI when the request is mainly metric diagnosis, pricing, GTM, or implementation planning without UX evidence; route to the narrower Productize skill that owns that artifact instead. Do not use it when the user lacks enough context to distinguish facts, assumptions, and decision risk.
output_artifact: Acceptance criteria for UI UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks
framework_fit: Acceptance criteria for UI fits Design work in the Design lifecycle when the user needs Acceptance criteria for UI UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks and has enough product context to make tradeoffs explicit. Use it to produce a decision-ready artifact, not a framework tour.
failure_modes: Produces Acceptance criteria for UI UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks without tying recommendations to the user's Design stage, evidence, and decision pressure.
Treats Acceptance criteria for UI as generic Design advice instead of naming assumptions, missing inputs, risks, and the next validation step.
Fails to route away from Acceptance criteria for UI when another Productize skill owns the requested artifact more directly.

examples.prompt: Use $acceptance-criteria-for-ui to turn this acceptance context into a decision-ready Acceptance criteria for UI UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks.
examples.expected_artifact: Acceptance criteria for UI UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks
